In conformity with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules ("the Rules") provision 5.6.1, the Company would like to notify the market that as at 22 July 2026 its issued capital comprised 244,528,950 ordinary shares of £0.05 each, excluding 54,163,655 shares held in treasury.

Commenting on the markets, Roland Arnold, representing the Investment Manager noted:
During June the Company’s NAV per share returned -1.4% to 1,496.87p on a total return basis, while our benchmark index, the Deutsche Numis Smaller Companies plus AIM (excluding Investment Companies) Index, fell by -2.6%.
UK equity markets delivered mixed returns during June, against a backdrop of increased volatility in global markets. Stronger-than-expected US economic data prompted investors to scale back expectations for interest rate cuts, while market leadership broadened beyond the largest US technology stocks as investors became more selective following their strong year-to-date performance.
Within the UK, the FTSE 100 Index outperformed the more domestically focused small and mid-cap segments. Political uncertainty increased following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's resignation and the subsequent Labour leadership contest, contributing to a higher risk premium being applied to UK domestic equities despite an improving economic backdrop. The Bank of England left interest rates unchanged as inflation continued to moderate, although expectations that global interest rates would remain higher for longer continued to weigh on investor sentiment.
Positive contributors during the month came from a broad range of companies that have continued to deliver.
Tatton Asset Management
, the discretionary fund management and model portfolio solutions provider to UK financial advisers, was a top contributor in the month. FY26 results demonstrated the continued strength of the business, with accelerating net inflows, ongoing market share gains and profits ahead of expectations. As a result, consensus earnings forecasts were upgraded by around 10%, helping to drive further share price appreciation.
Great Portland Estates
contributed positively to performance during the mo

latest.title: Transaction in Own Shares
latest.content:
Today 17:41
BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust Plc - Transaction in Own Shares
BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust Plc - Transaction in Own Shares
PR Newswire
LONDON, United Kingdom, July 22
BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc (‘the Company’)
LEI – 549300MS535KC2WH4082
Transaction in own shares: purchase of own shares to be held in treasury
The Company announces that it has today purchased 75,000 of its Ordinary Shares at an average price of 273.00 pence per share to be held in treasury.
Following settlement of this purchase on 24 July 2026 the issued share capital of the Company will be 244,378,950 Ordinary Shares, excluding 54,313,655 shares which are held in treasury.
Shares held in treasury do not carry any voting rights; 18.18% of the Company’s total issued share capital (298,692,605 Ordinary Shares, including treasury shares) will be held in treasury following settlement.
For reporting purposes under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules the market should exclude any shares held in treasury and should use the figure of 244,378,950 following settlement when determining if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company.
All enquiries:
Graham Venables
Company Secretary
BlackRock Investment Management (UK) Limited
Tel: 0203
649
3432
22 July 2026
Release

company_30s.what_they_do: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust is an investment trust that picks a portfolio of UK small‑cap companies, aiming to deliver growth through active selection and BlackRock’s research backing.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Small‑cap UK equities often grow faster than larger peers, and the trust offers exposure to that segment with a professional team that can tap into niche opportunities BlackRock’s global reach helps surface.
company_30s.current_state: The trust is currently trading well below its net asset value, reflecting a steep discount that many investors see as a buying opportunity. The portfolio is being refreshed as part of a recent tender offer, signalling a potential stabilisation of holdings.

rns_narrative: The trust has just closed a tender offer, setting a new price for shares and confirming the value of its holdings.
A portfolio update and net‑asset‑value report have been issued, giving fresh insight into the underlying companies and the trust’s valuation.
Recent filings about voting rights and own‑share transactions show the board is actively managing the capital structure.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are waiting for the tender price to settle before deciding.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the large discount to NAV and the trust’s long‑term track record, suggesting that a recovery to even half its peak is possible.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices warn that the discount could widen if the market remains weak and that there is still no clear catalyst beyond the tender.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as the tender offer closed, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning the market is not currently talking about the stock – a perfect condition for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The official tender price announcement will set a new support level and could trigger a buying spree if it is attractive.
A forthcoming net‑asset‑value release could confirm the trust’s valuation and reinforce the discount narrative.
Any dividend declaration or share buy‑back would provide an immediate upside signal.
Sector‑wide small‑cap rally or a positive BlackRock policy update could lift the entire trust.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that eclipses the average 3‑month volume, a break above the most recent swing high, or a clear endorsement from the board in a subsequent filing.
expect_next.timing_regime: The trust sits at the early stage of a recovery cycle – the crash has been deep, the tender offer has capped the lowest point, and the APEX compression suggests a tightening of the price range. Confirmation will come when price starts to consolidate above the new support level.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The trust is trading at a very steep discount, and the recent tender offer has capped the lowest price. Historically, deep crashes of this magnitude have delivered 3‑plus‑times returns once the market recognises the underlying value. If the price moves to roughly half its peak, the upside is more than triple the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear catalyst beyond the tender, the discount could widen if the small‑cap sector stays weak. The worst case is a further slide to the current level, which would wipe out any upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You can lose only the current price if the trend fails to reverse; the potential gain is over 3‑fold. The asymmetry is clearly in your favour.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the recent high and volume exceeds the 3‑month average. Allocate 10 % of your discretionary equity, set a stop a few points below the new support level, and trail the stop as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the tender price is announced and volume spikes. A break above the 20‑day moving average after the announcement is a strong confirmation to enter.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting, bankruptcy or fraud risks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deeply discounted small‑cap trust that has just capped its crash with a tender offer – if it recovers to half its peak, the upside is more than three times the current price, making it a clear contrarian bet.
